prompt: A moody surreal painting of a woman leaning forward through an old window frame, shown from behind and cropped tightly so the composition centers on her wildly windblown dark brown with copper highlights hair streaming left across the image in long tangled strands. Her bare shoulders and upper back are mostly hidden by her hair, and both hands rest on a worn wooden windowsill at the bottom center, with 2 visible fingers on each side gripping the ledge. The face is completely obscured by hair and angle. Behind her is a weathered multi-pane window with 6 rectangular panes in a 3-by-2 grid, painted in cold gray-blue tones with grime, scratches, drips, and distressed edges. Use an expressive mixed-media style combining charcoal, ink, dry brush, scratched lines, and textured oil paint on rough canvas, with sketchy white and black linework layered over muted industrial colors. The mood is introspective, stormy, and haunting, as if wind or emotion is pulling the figure apart. Soft diffused light, subtle contrast, painterly realism fused with abstract expressionism, square composition.
